_note: Feature tables for the horror classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: Action: fiends, undead, and spirits within 30 ft save (WIS) or must move away from you for 1 minute. WIS-mod uses per long rest.
Your weapon attacks count as magical against fiends, undead, and spirits.

2.d: Casts cleric-list spells as rites using WIS; a holy symbol or ritual kit is the focus.

5.d: 10 minutes of ritual wards a 30-ft area for 8 hours; hostile spirits entering it are visible and take 1d8 radiant on entry.

6.d: Recognize possession, hauntings, and curses on sight, and learn the entity's name category and goal.

7.d: You and allies within 10 ft have advantage on saves against fear and possession.

9.d: 1/long rest, a spirit or fiend that fails against your Rite of Repulsion is also restrained for 1 minute.

10.d: Your Rite of Repulsion affects creatures immune to being frightened.

11.d: Rite of Repulsion damage: affected creatures take 2d8 radiant when it takes hold.

13.d: 1/turn, +2d8 radiant damage on a weapon hit against fiends, undead, or spirits.

14.d: Immune to being frightened; advantage against charm.

15.d: 1/long rest, a 1-minute ritual expels a possessing entity from a host (contested WIS, you have advantage).

17.d: Resistance to necrotic and psychic damage.

18.d: 1/long rest, when an ally within 30 ft drops to 0 HP, they instead drop to WIS-mod x 5 HP.

20.d: 1/long rest, banish a fiend, undead, or spirit of CR 15 or lower to its home plane (CHA save negates).

3.d: Bonus action: a warding light for 1 minute; allies in 10 ft add +1d4 to saves against the supernatural.
Your Sanctified Ground ritual takes 1 minute instead of 10.

7.d: Your light reveals invisible and ethereal creatures within 10 ft.

11.d: While your Vigil burns, you and allies in it cannot be possessed and resist necrotic damage.

15.d: 1/long rest, when you would drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 instead and your Vigil reignites at double radius.

1.d: Double proficiency bonus on Arcana and Religion checks.
1 minute of chalk-work wards a doorway or 5-ft line for 8 hours; the first hostile creature crossing takes 2d6 psychic (INT save halves).

2.d: Casts warlock-list spells as invocations using INT; the grimoire is the focus.

5.d: Knowing a creature's true or use-name gives you advantage on your first spell attack or its first save against you each combat.

6.d: 1/rest, see through the eyes of a creature you can see for 1 minute (WIS save negates).

7.d: Advantage on saves against madness, charm, and fear from aberrations and fiends.

9.d: Your Warding Sigil can cover a 15-ft radius and also blocks ethereal passage.

10.d: Learn two rituals from any spell list; cast them as rituals only.

11.d: 1/long rest, when you expend your last spell slot, immediately regain it.

13.d: Learn one 6th-level warlock spell; cast it 1/long rest without a slot.

14.d: You cannot be surprised by aberrations, fiends, or undead.

15.d: Resistance to psychic damage; your thoughts cannot be read unless you allow it.

17.d: Learn one 7th-level warlock spell; cast it 1/long rest without a slot.

18.d: 1/long rest, reroll any one die you or a creature within 60 ft just rolled; the reroll stands.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast any warlock spell of 5th level or lower without a slot, twinned to two targets.

3.d: See invisible and ethereal creatures within 30 ft.
1/rest, an ally within 30 ft ends one charm, fear, or madness effect on itself.

7.d: 1/long rest, close a portal, summoning, or breach of CR/level 5 or lower as an action.

11.d: 1/long rest, step through the Veil: teleport 60 ft to a space you can see.

15.d: Creatures cannot teleport into or out of a 30-ft radius around you unless you allow it.

1.d: Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a bonus action, CON-mod uses per short rest.
1/day, when you drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 instead.

2.d: 1/rest, find one mundane useful item nearby (a weapon, keys, bandages); the DM decides what plausibly exists.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Advantage on initiative; on your first turn, learn the most immediate danger in the scene.

7.d: 1/short rest, halve the damage of one hit against you.

9.d: Won't Die Here recharges on a long rest and also ends one condition on you.

10.d: You cannot be surprised while conscious.

11.d: While below half HP, +2 to attack rolls and STR/DEX checks.

13.d: Allies within 10 ft may use your Adrenaline Surge movement options as a reaction when you do, 1/rest each.

14.d: Immune to being frightened while any ally can see you.

15.d: Regain all Adrenaline Surge uses when you roll initiative with none left.

17.d: Advantage on death saves; stabilizing you takes no check.

18.d: 1/long rest, take two full turns in the first round of combat.

20.d: Won't Die Here works 2/day, and each use also restores CON-mod x 5 HP.

3.d: While no ally is within 10 ft, +1 AC and advantage on Stealth.
Learn the exact HP of any creature you can see that is below half.

7.d: 1/long rest, gain temp HP equal to twice your level when combat starts.

11.d: Your attacks deal +1d8 damage against creatures that have downed an ally this fight.

15.d: While you are the last conscious party member, resist all damage and your speed doubles.

1.d: After seeing a creature act, learn one of its damage vulnerabilities, resistances, or special defenses.
Your weapon attacks count as silvered and magical for overcoming resistance.

2.d: Craft stakes, salt lines, and wardings during a rest; a warding gives one ally +2 AC against one monster type for a night.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Advantage against poison, disease, and curses.

7.d: Bonus action: mark a creature; your attacks against it deal +1d6 damage until it dies.

9.d: Advantage on saves against fear; allies within 10 ft add +1d4 to fear saves.

10.d: You can track any creature that has killed within the last week, even across water or rooftops.

11.d: Quarry damage rises to +2d6; marking is free when a creature harms an ally.

13.d: 1/short rest, declare a called shot: on hit, the target also loses one movement mode, attack, or sense until your next turn.

14.d: +1 AC; opportunity attacks against you have disadvantage.

15.d: Your critical hits against your Quarry deal maximum damage.

17.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your attacks ignore resistance and immunity to nonmagical damage.

18.d: You need no sleep during a hunt; a short rest gives you the benefits of a long rest 1/week.

20.d: 1/turn against your Quarry, reroll a missed attack; Quarry marks two creatures at once.

3.d: Your Hunter's Kit wardings cover the whole party; craft two per rest.
Advantage on checks to identify or recall lore about undead and shapechangers.

7.d: Your critical hits against undead or shapechangers stun them until the end of your next turn (CON save negates).

11.d: Your Silvered Strikes deal +1d8 radiant against undead, fiends, and shapechangers.

15.d: 1/long rest, an attack against a creature at half HP or lower becomes an automatic critical if it hits.

1.d: Your kit detects the unnatural: as an action, learn whether the supernatural is present within 60 ft and its rough direction.
Advantage on saves against charm and fear.

2.d: Casts wizard-list spells as calibrated apparatus effects using INT; the kit is the focus.

5.d: After 1 minute of measurement, grant the party advantage on the next save any of them makes against a haunting or psychic effect.

6.d: 1/rest, when an ally fails an INT, WIS, or CHA save within 30 ft, let them reroll with your guidance.

7.d: Your Field Instruments now reveal the entity's type and whether it is hostile.

10.d: Advantage on checks to identify supernatural creatures and effects you have measured before.

11.d: 1/long rest, a 10-minute setup makes a 20-ft radius immune to teleportation and phasing for 1 hour.

13.d: 1/rest, after a creature you have measured attacks, impose disadvantage on that attack.

14.d: Immune to being frightened while your instruments are in hand.

15.d: Your apparatus spells ignore resistance to psychic and force damage.

17.d: Learn three spells from any spell list; they count as wizard-list apparatus effects for you.

18.d: 1/long rest, force an invisible, ethereal, or shapechanged creature within 60 ft into its true visible form for 1 minute (CHA save negates).

20.d: 1/long rest, your next apparatus spell is cast at maximum effect and cannot be resisted by legendary resistance.

3.d: 1/long rest, a 10-minute session lets the party ask a lingering spirit three questions; it answers truthfully but briefly.
Advantage on Investigation checks in places where someone died violently.

7.d: After damaging a spirit, your party's attacks against it count as magical for 1 minute.

11.d: 1/long rest, compel a spirit within 30 ft to manifest visibly and audibly for 10 minutes.

15.d: 1/long rest, you and up to three allies see and strike into the Ethereal Plane for 1 minute.

1.d: Casts sorcerer-list spells as stigmata using CHA; casting leaves visible marks on your skin.
You recognize members, symbols, and workings of your former cult on sight.

2.d: 1/rest, cast a 1st-level stigmata without a slot; take 1d4 necrotic that cannot be reduced.

5.d: 2/rest, cast a cantrip as a bonus action after casting a leveled stigmata.

7.d: Advantage on initiative when cultists or their summonings are present; you cannot be surprised by them.

9.d: 1/rest, reaction when hit: gain resistance to that damage type until your next turn.

11.d: Bleeding Power can cast up to 3rd-level stigmata; the backlash is 2d4 necrotic.

13.d: Advantage on saves against spells cast by fiends, cultists, and aberrations.

15.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your CHA modifier when you drop below half HP.

17.d: Reaction when a creature damages you with a spell: it takes your CHA mod + level in necrotic damage.

18.d: Immune to charm and possession by fiends and aberrations.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast one stigmata of 5th level or lower without a slot at its maximum effect.

1.d: 1/turn, add your CHA modifier as necrotic damage to a damaging stigmata.

6.d: 1/long rest, duplicate a stigmata of 2nd level or lower you just cast at a second target for free.

10.d: While below half HP, +2 AC and your stigmata ignore resistance to necrotic.

14.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your stigmata cost no verbal or somatic components and cannot be counterspelled.
